world.title: Africa’s Economy Shows Resilience in 2026 but Faces Debt and Growth Challenges
world.description: Africa’s economy is recovering steadily, with growth improving and inflation easing, but progress remains uneven and constrained by high debt and structural challenges. Sustained growth and real development will depend on reforms, investment, and better economic management to turn resilience into long-term prosperity.

world.title: Radisson Hotel Group Crosses 100 Hotels in Africa, Expands Footprint Across Key Markets
world.description: Radisson Hotel Group crosses 100 hotels in Africa, expanding across key markets with strong pipeline growth and conversion-led strategy.

world.title: Nigerian worshippers celebrate Easter under tight security after deadly attack

world.title: How US forces pulled off daring rescue of stranded airman shot down in Iran
world.description: After an American fighter jet was shot down in a mountainous area of Iran, the US military pulled off an incredible rescue of two airmen, living up to its credo of "no man left behind", despite running into difficulty.

world.title: Iran warns of 'devastating' retaliation after Trump's expletive-filled threats
world.description: The US president says he will strike Iran's power plants and bridges if Tehran does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz before an extended deadline expires.

world.title: US-Israel-Iran War Live: 15 sites hit as Iran launches fresh attacks targeting central Israel
world.description: US-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: US President Donald Trump has escalated tensions with Iran, warning that "power plants and bridges" could be targeted if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened by Tuesday evening, saying "Tuesday will be Power Plant Day, and Bridge Day, all wrapped up in one." Trump’s abusive threat drew a sharp from Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, who warned that Trump’s "reckless moves" risk dragging the country into living hell and insisting "you won’t gain anything through war crimes." Behind the scenes, Oman has held urgent talks with Iran to ensure smooth flow of transit through war-hit Hormuz, while Pakistan and Egypt have kept diplomatic channels open between Washington and Tehran. At the same time, violence has intensified elsewhere, with Israeli strikes in Beirut killing at least four people and Hezbollah launching retaliatory attacks, escalating the conflict further. Follow IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

business.title: Iran war hits India's star investors: Ashish Kacholia, Mukul Agrawal, others see 90% of their stocks lose in 2026
business.description: Geopolitical tensions stemming from the Iran conflict triggered a broad market sell-off in the March quarter, significantly impacting India's star investors. Portfolios of Ashish Kacholia, Mukul Agrawal, and others saw a vast majority of their stocks decline, with many experiencing losses exceeding 90% of their holdings. This downturn highlighted the vulnerability of mid- and small-cap stocks during global uncertainty.

science.description: The Apollo 13 crew has held the distance record for a manned aircraft's maximum range from Earth since the 1970s. The Artemis II crew is about to change that.
